projectDescription: XMage is an open-source Magic: The Gathering game engine that lets you play against human opponents or computer AI opponents with full rules enforcement for over 31,000 unique cards.
keyFeatures: Full rules enforcement for 31,000+ unique Magic: The Gathering cards
Play against human opponents online or computer AI opponents
Support for diverse game formats: Commander, Vintage, Legacy, Modern, Pioneer, Standard, Pauper, Oathbreaker, Tiny Leaders, Momir, and more
Booster Draft and Sealed Deck tournament support (elimination and Swiss) with 2–16 players
Multiple AI opponent types: Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS), look-ahead (MA), and draft bots
Full-featured deck editor with support for many deck formats and external deck sources
Client-server architecture supporting public servers and self-hosted private servers
Extensible plugin system for game types, player types, tournament types, and deck validators
publicApiSurface: MageServer interface — server-side RPC commands (auth, game, tournament, chat)
Card / CardImpl — base card abstraction used for all card implementations
Ability / AbilityImpl — ability interface hierarchy (activated, triggered, static, loyalty)
Effect interfaces — ContinuousEffect, OneShotEffect, ReplacementEffect, etc.
Game / GameImpl — game-state and rules-enforcement engine
GameImpl game modes — TwoPlayerDuel, CommanderDuel, FreeForAll, BrawlDuel, OathbreakerDuel, MomirDuel, etc.
DeckValidator / DeckValidatorFactory — format legality checking (Standard, Modern, Legacy, Commander, etc.)
ExpansionSet / Sets — card set and card database registry
ComputerPlayer / ComputerPlayer6 / ComputerPlayerMCTS — AI player implementations
config.xml — server configuration (port, maxGameThreads, authenticationActivated, player/game/tournament plugin registration)
Utils scripts — gen-card.pl, mtg-cards-data-scryfall.py, version-bump.pl card-generation utilities
